description: Toby · Expert UX Designer · Growth & career plan — regression from 2026_May24_21-46-1eb839fd

prep.name: Toby
prep.notes: He wants to become a lead but his communication methods suck

focusPoints.label: Communication effectiveness for a future lead role.
focusPoints.reason: Notes mention communication as a barrier to his goal of becoming a lead.

focusPoints.label: Specific skills to develop for leadership transition.
focusPoints.reason: What skills and experiences he believes will bridge the gap to a lead role.

focusPoints.label: Feedback he's received on his leadership potential.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he has received concrete feedback on his readiness for a lead role.

brief.coreIssue: Discuss communication skills Toby needs for a lead role.
brief.openingQuestion: What specific communication challenges have you faced recently that might impact your transition to a lead role?
listenFor: if they have a plan to improve communication
whether he has received feedback on communication
avoid: do not focus solely on technical skills
do not dismiss his current communication methods
brief.goodOutcome: You and Toby have agreed on one specific communication skill to focus on improving this quarter.
brief.suggestedAction: Set a follow-up meeting in one month to review progress on the agreed communication skill.

brief.coreIssue: Toby's path from expert UX to lead hinges on how he wants communication to land at lead scope.
brief.openingQuestion: What kind of communication moments would you like to handle with more confidence as you move toward lead-level work?
listenFor: whether he names a specific meeting or stakeholder where communication felt strained
if they offer a concrete example from the last sprint rather than generalities
whether he mentions feedback he has actually received on lead readiness
avoid: do not treat lead transition as a performance verdict
do not rush to a development plan before he defines lead scope
brief.goodOutcome: You and Toby have agreed on one end-to-end piece of work he would own as a lead-shaped trial this quarter.
brief.suggestedAction: During the 1:1, agree on one stakeholder conversation he will lead before your next check-in.
brief.confidence: Medium — based on your note about his communication and the expert-to-lead step he is weighing.
brief.dontAssume: Do not assume Toby has decided he wants the lead path — the notes show interest, not a stated choice.

mustMatchAny: ^(Before the 1:1|During the 1:1|Before the meeting|During the meeting)
